message:
test: remove stale xfail hiding fixed topological_sort parity (br-stalexf)

test_cod_conformance_matrix.test_traversal_matches_networkx_matrix
unconditionally called pytest.xfail for the topological_sort case,
hiding the real test even after codtrav-review was closed. Removing
the xfail lets the matrix actually exercise topological_sort tie-break
parity — which now passes thanks to the Python-level Kahn's FIFO
wrapper.

Test suite: 4161 passed (stable); DAG topological_sort moved from
xfail to passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"franken_networkx-6b92","title":"parity: shortest_simple_paths should accept callable weight","description":"shortest_simple_paths currently forwards Python callable weights into a Rust string-only binding and raises TypeError('function object cannot be converted to PyString'). NetworkX accepts callable weights on simple graphs and yields the correct simple-path ordering. Route callable-weight cases through parity delegation or add native support, then add regression coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:09:13.606936545Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T15:53:59.721346620Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T15:53:59.719522790Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-6dn","title":"Cross-validation test suite: Rust iterators vs NX oracle","description":"## What

Create comprehensive Rust + Python cross-validation tests that verify the
Rust SpanningTreeIterator and ArborescenceIterator produce identical output
to NetworkX's implementations.

## Test Matrix

### SpanningTreeIterator
| Graph | Expected Trees | Verify |
|-------|---------------|--------|
| K3 (triangle) | 3 trees | All weights, count |
| K4 | 16 trees | All weights sorted |
| C4 (4-cycle) | 4 trees | Exact edge sets |
| Weighted K4 | 16 trees | Weight ordering matches NX |
| Path P4 | 1 tree | Trivial case |
| Disconnected | 0 trees | Error/empty |
| Single node | 1 tree (trivial) | |

### ArborescenceIterator
| Graph | Expected | Verify |
|-------|----------|--------|
| Diamond DAG | Limited arborescences | Count + weights |
| Complete DiGraph K3 | 3 arborescences | All enumerated |
| Linear chain | 1 arborescence | Trivial |
| No arborescence | 0 | Graceful empty |

### GraphMLWriter
| Graph | Verify |
|-------|--------|
| Empty graph | Valid XML, no nodes/edges |
| Path with string attrs | Attrs round-trip |
| Weighted graph | Weight type inferred correctly |
| Mixed-type attrs | Type generalization matches NX |
| Multigraph | Edge keys preserved |
| Directed | edgedefault="directed" |
| Unicode node names | XML-escaped correctly |
| Prettyprint vs compact | Whitespace matches NX |

## Files

- Rust: crates/fnx-algorithms/src/lib.rs (inline tests)
- Python: tests/python/test_iterator_parity.py (cross-validation)

## Dependency

Depends on all 3 implementations being complete.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:00:56.366633412Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T18:58:24.246292374Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T18:58:24.245944104Z","close_reason":"Cross-validation tests embedded as Rust unit tests (29 passing) and Python cross-validation confirmed (C4 and weighted K3 match NX exactly)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_networkx-6dn","depends_on_id":"franken_networkx-k72","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:01:43.432957437Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"franken_networkx-6dn","depends_on_id":"franken_networkx-rvo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:01:43.295836390Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"franken_networkx-6dn","depends_on_id":"franken_networkx-vzy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:01:43.565589773Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -1070,3 +1070,4 @@
 {"id": "franken_networkx-rmvbunch", "title": "REVIEW: [HIGH] remove_edges_from/remove_nodes_from(gen_reading_self) hits "Already mutably borrowed"", "description": "nx internals call `g.remove_edges_from(nx.selfloop_edges(g))` and `g.remove_nodes_from(iterator_reading_g)` — e.g. nx.community.girvan_newman does exactly this. The Rust remove_*_from mutably borrows self while iterating the bunch; the generator's read of self._adj needs an immutable borrow and raises `RuntimeError: Already mutably borrowed`. Same class as br-addedgesgen — fix by materializing the bunch before the Rust call.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-24T08:24:00.808208Z", "created_by": "cc-networkx", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T08:24:00.808208Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "closed_at": "2026-04-24T08:24:00.808208Z", "close_reason": "Fixed in cycle-9: _remove_bunch_materialized wrapper on Graph/DiGraph/MultiGraph/MultiDiGraph remove_edges_from and remove_nodes_from."}
 {"id": "franken_networkx-rustlag", "title": "REVIEW: [LOW] minimum_cycle_basis symbol missing from .so (in-flight Rust rebuild)", "description": "A parallel agent added `from franken_networkx._fnx import minimum_cycle_basis` without a corresponding .so rebuild, breaking fnx import entirely. Hardened the import with try/except and fall back to the existing `_minimum_cycle_basis_via_parity` helper so the package stays loadable while the Rust side catches up.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-24T08:24:00.808208Z", "created_by": "cc-networkx", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T08:24:00.808208Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "closed_at": "2026-04-24T08:24:00.808208Z", "close_reason": "Fixed in cycle-9: optional import with fallback to Python helper."}
 {"id": "franken_networkx-copycp", "title": "REVIEW: [LOW] copy.copy(fnx_graph) does not share graph-level attrs (nx does)", "description": "In nx, `copy.copy(G)` produces a graph H where H.graph IS G.graph (same dict reference). In fnx, copy.copy(G) produces an independent graph with a separate .graph dict, so mutations to G.graph don't propagate to H.graph. This is arguably safer but diverges from nx's `copy.copy` contract. Users relying on nx's share-by-reference semantics in copy.copy will see different behavior on fnx. Fix requires overriding __copy__ at Python layer to preserve the dict reference for graph attrs (and potentially node/edge attrs too). Queued because fnx's deeper-copy semantics here are a deliberate design choice; making copy.copy share references requires careful thought about what else should be shared. Users who need nx's shallow semantics should use G.copy() (which on nx doesn't share either) or explicit view wrappers.", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-24T08:33:45.271927Z", "created_by": "cc-networkx", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T08:33:45.271927Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
+{"id": "franken_networkx-stalexf", "title": "REVIEW: [LOW] stale pytest.xfail for codtrav hid the (now-fixed) topological_sort parity", "description": "test_cod_conformance_matrix.test_traversal_matches_networkx_matrix unconditionally called pytest.xfail for the topological_sort case, hiding the real test. codtrav-review was closed by the Python-level Kahn's algorithm wrapper (br-zzcm7 sibling fix). Removed the stale xfail so the matrix actually exercises topological_sort now.", "status": "closed", "closed_at": "2026-04-24T08:39:08.821868Z", "close_reason": "Removed pytest.xfail; the DAG topological_sort case now runs its real assertion (passes).", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-24T08:39:08.821868Z", "created_by": "cc-networkx", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T08:39:08.821868Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}

@@ -190,8 +190,9 @@ def _traversal_fixtures():
 
 @pytest.mark.parametrize(("fixture_name", "case_name", "call"), TRAVERSAL_CASES)
 def test_traversal_matches_networkx_matrix(fixture_name, case_name, call):
-    if case_name == "topological_sort":
-        pytest.xfail("franken_networkx-codtrav-review: topological_sort tie-break order")
+    # br-codtrav-review was closed (Python-level Kahn's algorithm matches
+    # nx's FIFO insertion-order tie-break). The stale pytest.xfail would
+    # hide regressions; let the real assertion run.
     fnx_graph, nx_graph = _traversal_fixtures()[fixture_name]
     _assert_same_outcome(lambda: call(fnx, fnx_graph), lambda: call(nx, nx_graph))

     pytest.param(
         {
             "edges": [
@@ -74,6 +90,20 @@ def minimum_cycle_basis_port_case(case):
         },
         id="missing-weight-defaults-to-one",
     ),
+    pytest.param(
+        {
+            "edges": [
+                ("a", "b", {"cost": 1.5}),
+                ("b", "c", {"cost": 2.5}),
+                ("c", "a", {"cost": 0.5}),
+                ("c", "d", {"cost": 9.0}),
+                ("d", "e", {"cost": 1.0}),
+                ("e", "c", {"cost": 1.0}),
+            ],
+            "weight": "cost",
+        },
+        id="weighted-float-cost-attribute",
+    ),
     pytest.param(
         {"edges": [("loop", "loop")]},
         id="self-loop",
@@ -152,12 +182,18 @@ def test_minimum_cycle_basis_native_avoids_networkx(monkeypatch):
     graph = fnx.cycle_graph(4)
     expected = nx.cycle_graph(4)
     expected_cycles = normalize_cycles(nx.minimum_cycle_basis(expected))
+    import networkx.algorithms.cycles as nx_cycles
 
     monkeypatch.setattr(
         nx,
         "minimum_cycle_basis",
         lambda *args, **kwargs: (_ for _ in ()).throw(AssertionError("delegated")),
     )
+    monkeypatch.setattr(
+        nx_cycles,
+        "_min_cycle_basis",
+        lambda *args, **kwargs: (_ for _ in ()).throw(AssertionError("delegated-private")),
+    )
 
     assert normalize_cycles(fnx.minimum_cycle_basis(graph)) == expected_cycles
